[[BLOCK:slide53_shape0]]
•   “Individuals with Type 2 diabetes, irrespective of blood sugar control, often exhibit elevated triglycerides (Triglycerides (TCH)), reduced HDL (High-density lipoprotein cholesterol), and occasionally increased LDL (Low-density lipoprotein cholesterol)… The American Diabetes Association advises monitoring cholesterol levels at least annually, or more frequently if levels are elevated and not managed with medication. For individuals with diabetes but without diagnosed coronary heart disease, it is recommended that LDL levels remain below 100 milligrams per deciliter (mg/dL), HDL levels exceed 50 mg/dL, and triglycerides stay below 150 mg/dL.”
•   Source: https://www.verywellhealth.com/the-dangers-of-high-cholesterol-and-diabetes-3571713
